items.scenario: You are a third-year sociology major who tried to register for a required research methods course this morning but discovered the system listed it as 'closed' even though the printed catalog said 30 seats remained. You need this course to graduate on time and are not sure who to contact.
items.direction: Write an email to the department's registration coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the registration error you encountered this morning.
Explain why the course is required for your graduation.
Ask whether the coordinator can add you manually.
items.subject: Help registering for SOC 305 research methods

items.scenario: You have spent two weeks using a new time-tracking system that your manager rolled out at the small marketing agency where you work part-time. You like several features but have noticed two issues that are slowing down the team's daily check-ins.
items.direction: Write an email to your manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your manager for selecting the new system.
Describe the issues you have noticed in daily check-ins.
Suggest a small change that could fix them.
items.subject: Two small issues with the new time-tracking tool

items.scenario: You are a frequent visitor at the Riverview Community Library, which recently moved its weekend opening time from 9 AM to 11 AM. You have noticed that many parents and young children, who used to come for the morning reading program, no longer attend.
items.direction: Write an email to the library director. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain when you noticed the change in attendance.
Describe the impact on families who used the morning program.
Suggest restoring the earlier weekend opening time.
items.subject: Weekend attendance has dropped since the schedule change

items.scenario: Your roommate Daniela invited you to a long-planned hiking trip to Pine Ridge State Park this Saturday, but you just received an unexpected callback for a scholarship interview at the same time. You want to keep both the friendship and the trip alive.
items.direction: Write an email to your roommate. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you can no longer go on Saturday.
Thank Daniela for organizing the trip.
Suggest other weekends when you could reschedule the hike.
items.subject: Sorry — need to reschedule Saturday's hike to Pine Ridge
